title: Monogramma robusta
intro: Monogramma robusta is a species of plant belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the robust rock-cap fern. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Monogramma robusta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Monogramma robusta is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and skin disorders, as well as to boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Monogramma robusta is classified as a species of fern, specifically in the genus Monogramma, which belongs to the family Pteridaceae. This genus is part of the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The family Pteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the maidenhair ferns, and they are found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. The class Polypodiopsida is a group of ferns that are vascular plants and are known for their unique life cycle. Monogramma robusta is a species of fern that is native to tropical regions and has distinctive leaves with a unique shape.
uses: Monogramma robusta has been found to possess various medicinal properties, although its traditional uses are not well-documented.
